| 7 | 1. Real-world verification architecture (1/3) | 0:38 | 1. Real-world verification architecture (1/3). DMA path: CPU/config → descriptor ring → bus master → memory/peripheral Protocol path: serial frame → byte stream → transaction → scoreboard VIP encapsulates protocol knowledge for reuse across projects and modules module7/dut/dma/simple_dma.v and dut/protocols/uart.v anchor the labs Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 8 | 1. Real-world verification architecture (2/3) | 0:28 | 1. Real-world verification architecture (2/3). DMA agent: Programs descriptors; monitors transfer completion Protocol agents (UART/SPI/I2C): Bus-specific driver/monitor/sequencer sets VIP package: Reusable agent + sequences + config for a protocol Scoreboard / predictor: End-to-end data integrity across DMA + peripheral |

DMA: descriptor alignment, boundary lengths, error flags, concurrent channels Serial protocols: baud/bit timing, framing, parity, back-to-back packets VIP usage: configure via object; run standard sequences before custom stress Directed tests prove basic paths; random tests explore corner combinations Refer to the diagram on the right. |
| 24 | 2. Production best practices (1/2) | 0:38 | 2. Production best practices (1/2). Centralize plusargs via CLP (Module 8) for seed, verbosity, test name Separate test intent (test class) from reusable env (VIP + env) Log protocol transactions at UVM_MEDIUM; errors at UVM_LOW Regression: ./scripts/module7.sh --all-examples --uvm-tests Refer to the diagram on the right. |

Parameters: None (randomizes all rand fields in object) Constraints: Use with { constraint_expression; } to limit values Example: len inside {[1:20]} constrains transfer length to 1-20 bytes Key: void'() suppresses Verilator warning about ignored return value From MODULE7 Topics Covered — protocol/design depth. |

Constraints: Use with { constraint; } to limit values Key: len inside {[1:20]} constrains transfer length Method Signature: task get_next_item(ref T item); - Get transaction from sequencer task item_done(); - Signal transaction completion |
| 58 | 1. DMA Verification (5/9) | 0:36 | 1. DMA Verification (5/9). Protocol Flow: Key: do-while loop waits for dma_done pulse (blocking wait) Timing: Start signal is one clock cycle pulse Blocking Behavior: get_next_item() blocks until sequencer provides transaction, item_done() unblocks sequencer Completion Detection: Wait for dma_done signal assertion (may take multiple clock cycles) |
| 59 | 1. DMA Verification (6/9) | 0:36 | 1. DMA Verification (6/9). Purpose: Generate and send multiple DMA transfers to sequencer Execution Flow: Key: repeat loop creates multiple transactions sequentially Blocking Behavior: start_item() blocks until sequencer grants access |
| 60 | 1. DMA Verification (7/9) | 0:36 | 1. DMA Verification (7/9). Transaction Lifetime: Created in loop Sent to sequencer via start_item() Driver processes it (drives signals, waits for DMA done) Driver signals completion via item_done() |
| 61 | 1. DMA Verification (8/9) | 0:36 | 1. DMA Verification (8/9). | 98 | 5. System-Level Verification (11/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (11/24). Validate configuration Final checks Setup verification Pattern: forever #period clk = ~clk; Purpose: Generate continuous clock signal |
| 99 | 5. System-Level Verification (12/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (12/24). Key: forever loop runs indefinitely Pattern: repeat(n) @(posedge clk); Purpose: Wait for n clock cycles Usage: Reset sequences, initialization delays Key: repeat loop with event control |
| 100 | 5. System-Level Verification (13/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (13/24). Purpose: Configure components before test starts Key: Use null context and "*" wildcard for global configuration Operator: @(event) - event control Purpose: Wait for event to occur Behavior: Blocks until event occurs |
| 101 | 5. System-Level Verification (14/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (14/24). Operator: #time - delay operator Purpose: Wait for specified time Behavior: Blocks for specified time units Usage: Clock generation, timing delays Operator: <= - non-blocking assignment |
| 102 | 5. System-Level Verification (15/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (15/24). Behavior: All non-blocking assignments in current time step execute together Usage: Clock-synchronized signal assignments Statement: repeat(n) statement; Purpose: Execute statement n times Behavior: Loops n times |
| 103 | 5. System-Level Verification (16/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (16/24). Statement: forever statement; Purpose: Execute statement indefinitely Behavior: Infinite loop Usage: Clock generation, continuous monitoring Function: $sformatf(format, args...) → string |
| 104 | 5. System-Level Verification (17/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (17/24). Return: Formatted string Usage: Logging, debugging, reporting Problem: Simulation hangs waiting for dma_done Solution: Check DUT implementation, add timeout Code: |
| 105 | 5. System-Level Verification (18/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (18/24). Problem: Simulation hangs waiting for tx_busy deassert Solution: Check DUT implementation, verify clock Code: Prevention: Add bounded wait with timeout checking Problem: print_topology() shows nothing |
| 106 | 5. System-Level Verification (19/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (19/24). Code: Must be after connect_phase() completes Check: Verify all components created in build_phase() Problem: VIP agent hardcoded for specific use Solution: Use ConfigDB for configuration, avoid hardcoded values Code: |

Solution: Use appropriate verbosity levels Code: UVM_MEDIUM for normal, UVM_HIGH for detailed Control: Set verbosity via +UVM_VERBOSITY=UVM_MEDIUM Problem: Simulation hangs, no progress Solution: Verify clock generation in module |
| 108 | 5. System-Level Verification (21/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (21/24). Check: Clock must be in initial block, not always block Problem: uvm_fatal("NO_VIF", ...) error Solution: Set interface before run_test() Code: Timing: ConfigDB set → run_test() → component build_phase() retrieves |
| 109 | 5. System-Level Verification (22/24) | 0:36 | 5. System-Level Verification (22/24). Solution: Verify reset sequence completes Code: Check: DUT dma_done signal implementation Check: Clock running correctly Check: Reset sequence completed |

| 131 | DMA Done Not Detected | 0:28 | DMA Done Not Detected. Problem: Simulation hangs waiting for dma_done Solution: Check DUT implementation, add timeout Code: Prevention: Add bounded wait with timeout checking |
| 132 | UART Busy Never Deasserts | 0:28 | UART Busy Never Deasserts. Problem: Simulation hangs waiting for tx_busy deassert Solution: Check DUT implementation, verify clock Code: Prevention: Add bounded wait with timeout checking |
| 133 | Topology Not Printed | 0:28 | Topology Not Printed. Problem: print_topology() shows nothing Solution: Call in end_of_elaboration_phase(), not earlier Code: Must be after connect_phase() completes Check: Verify all components created in build_phase() |
| 134 | VIP Agent Not Reusable | 0:24 | VIP Agent Not Reusable. Problem: VIP agent hardcoded for specific use Solution: Use ConfigDB for configuration, avoid hardcoded values Code: |
| 135 | Phase Logging Too Verbose | 0:28 | Phase Logging Too Verbose. Problem: Too many log messages Solution: Use appropriate verbosity levels Code: UVM_MEDIUM for normal, UVM_HIGH for detailed Control: Set verbosity via +UVM_VERBOSITY=UVM_MEDIUM |
| 136 | Clock Not Running | 0:28 | Clock Not Running. Problem: Simulation hangs, no progress Solution: Verify clock generation in module Code: Check: Clock must be in initial block, not always block |
| 137 | Practice & assessment | 0:08 | Next section: Practice & assessment. |

        ## Section narration (edit for TTS)

        - **Design architecture (Real-world verification architecture, VIP and reuse architecture, Module 7 application map):** Walk through the block diagram, then relate each block to files under module7/examples/.
- **Execution:** Explain make run / UVM make steps, then walk the artifact table and directed-test sequence slide by slide.
- **Verification (Application-specific verification strategy, Production best practices):** Explain what stimulus is applied, what is checked, and what is intentionally out of scope.
- **Syllabus:** Cover 5 topic section(s) — pause on protocol timing and signals.
- **Before exercises:** Ask learners to recall the learning outcomes slide; they should explain each bullet in their own words.
- **Hands-on:** Run module7/EXAMPLES.md labs; narrate expected PASS lines.

        ## Notes

        - Slides from **Before You Start**, **Design Architecture**, **Verification & Testing Methods**, **Topics Covered**, **EXAMPLES.md**, and **Learning Outcomes**.
        - Full detail: `docs/MODULE7.md` and `module7/EXAMPLES.md`.
        - Regenerate: `regenerate_course_outlines.sh <course_root> --module 7`
